I wasn’t trying to start a company. I just wanted to automate a frustrating workflow that was wasting hours every week. But after combining a handful of JavaScript libraries, that tiny project evolved into a product people were willing to pay for every month.

5 min read
2 hours ago

--

Photo by Kevin Canlas on Unsplash
1. The Problem That Refused to Stay Small
Every developer has that one annoying task.
The task you promise yourself you’ll automate someday.
Mine was collecting marketing data from multiple platforms, generating reports, and sending updates to clients.
Every week looked identical:

• Log into dashboards

• Export CSV files

• Clean data

• Create charts

• Email reports
It wasn’t difficult work.
It was repetitive work.
And repetitive work is usually an opportunity disguised as boredom.
